name: Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs)
summary: GANs are a framework for training generative models using an adversarial game between two neural networks: a generator that creates synthetic data and a discriminator that tries to distinguish real data from generated data. The generator improves by learning to fool the discriminator, while the discriminator improves by learning to detect fakes. This min-max game theoretically converges when the generator produces data indistinguishable from real data. Introduced by Ian Goodfellow in 2014, GANs dominated image generation until diffusion models surpassed them around 2021-2022. Key variants include DCGAN, StyleGAN, CycleGAN, and Pix2Pix.
query_hooks: How do generative adversarial networks work?
